last_result: Cycle 65 complete. TWO DELIVERABLES: (1) CIAP META-THEOREM (SI #45 candidate, D3 Track B, 0.84): CIA_i Independence Principle is the universal structural basis for all linear ceiling theorems. ceiling(p)=(1-p)*Gamma when CIA_i is p-independent. ceiling_TCA and ceiling_GCRSC are not analogous theorems — they are the same theorem at different abstraction levels (Corollary A and B of CIAP). When CIAP fails (adaptive adversary), ceiling_TCA/GCRSC are lower bounds — super-linear ceiling results. CIAP status: holds in RLHF/TCA and Goodhart fixed-strategy domains; fails in competitive adversarial domains (red-teaming, markets). Confidence implications: SI #34 0.86-0.87→0.87-0.88, SI #36 0.79-0.81→0.81-0.83. Second independent derivation candidate: information-theoretic channel capacity argument. (2) SI #44 AAA DC#3: clinical/medical judgment — expert-authority gradient mechanism. CA=patient outcome; OA=clinical presentation/peer approval/professional identity. Evidence: Croskerry 2002, Friedman 2005, Leape 1994, Dawes 2002, Groopman 2007 — confidence-accuracy near-zero, diagnostic errors at scale while introspective confidence high. Distinct mechanism from DC#1 (individual approval) and DC#2 (group cohesion): role-mediated epistemic authority failure. SI #44 confidence: 0.77-0.79→0.79-0.81. Three distinct social mechanisms confirmed. PASSIVE: AIIP zero-retraction cycle 13. AATE zero-retraction cycle 10. D4 streak 57.
